summary: Completed comprehensive testing of SmartMeal AI meal planning app after model name fix. Backend API fully functional (91.7% success rate), but critical frontend integration issue identified where UI gets stuck in loading state despite successful API responses.

minor_bugs.issue: Returns 200 instead of 401 when no authentication token provided
minor_bugs.impact: Minor security inconsistency - should return 401 for unauthenticated requests

critical_bugs.issue: Recipe search gets stuck in loading state despite successful API response
critical_bugs.user_impact: Users cannot see AI-generated recipes - core feature completely broken from UI perspective
critical_bugs.technical_details: Fetch request to /api/ai/suggest-recipes is made but response never processed by React component. API returns 200 with 3 recipes via curl, but frontend stays in loading state.

integration_issues.issue: Session cookie not automatically applied on page load - requires manual setting
integration_issues.user_impact: Users may need to re-authenticate frequently

passed_tests: Backend API endpoints working perfectly (11/12 tests passed)
AI recipe generation API returns 3 detailed recipes with all required fields
Authentication system working with session tokens
Dashboard loads correctly with user greeting and data summary
Orange/green theme implemented correctly across all pages
Meal Planner page loads and displays search interface
Quick suggestions buttons work and fill search input
Loading state displays correctly during search
Test user has existing pantry data (3 ingredients) and family data (1 member)
action_item_for_main_agent: CRITICAL: Fix frontend integration bug in MealPlanner.js - the handleSearch function's fetch request to /api/ai/suggest-recipes is not completing properly. The API works perfectly (confirmed via curl and direct browser fetch), but the React component's fetch call hangs and never reaches the response processing code. This prevents users from seeing AI recipe suggestions, making the core feature unusable.

success_percentage.frontend: 70% (UI/UX working well, but core AI feature broken due to fetch integration issue)
